title: geum aleppicum subvar. dissectum
intro: Geum aleppicum subvar. dissectum, also known as the cut-leaved avens, is a perennial plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, thanks to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Geum aleppicum subvar. dissectum can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and immunity.
